column1: SPEC Template — NEURONIUM AI (Commitment-aware AI Super Agent)

column2: Fill In
NEURONIUM AI — Commitment-aware AI Super Agent with Action Graph (DAG) planning, hybrid memory (GraphRAG + agentic retrieval), verification critics, typed contracts, and audit/replay trace

Build an open-source Python framework that converts a high-level user objective into a Hierarchical Task Network (HTN), compiles it into a deterministic Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) of typed nodes (ModelNode/CodeNode/McpToolNode), executes it with pause/stop/revise/replan control, persists every step into content-addressable storage and an index (SQLite/Postgres+pgvector), records a canonical trace, validates outputs through critic verifiers, and supports full offline replay. Scope covers: public Python API (AgentRunner), CLI (neuronium-agent), TOML configuration, FS CAS / SQLite local mode, Postgres + Redis + RQ production mode, MCP tool integration, GraphRAG-lite memory, Docker code sandbox.

Dataism Lab (open-source community); target adopters — AI engineering teams in mid/large enterprises, R&D labs, regulated industries (fintech, medtech, govtech) and AI agent platform builders that need auditable and reproducible agent execution

Modern LLM-agent frameworks (LangChain, AutoGen, CrewAI) are non-deterministic, hard to audit, opaque under failure, lack reproducible replay, mix planning with execution, and have no first-class commitment/control protocol. This blocks enterprise adoption where SLA, compliance, root-cause analysis and reproducibility are mandatory.

A layered framework that (a) separates Plan → Execute → Verify → Adapt, (b) compiles plans into typed DAGs with canonical JSON for byte-identical replay, (c) records every transition into an append-only trace with content-addressed artifacts, (d) exposes a declarative control protocol (continue/pause/revise/replan/stop/escalate) over a state machine, (e) provides critic-based verification before commit, (f) allows scaling local single-process runs to distributed Redis+RQ workers without code change.

1) Replay determinism rate ≥ 99% on identical seed/config (byte-equality of artifact_id). 2) Mean time to resume from PAUSED checkpoint ≤ 2s (local) / 10s (distributed). 3) Trace coverage = 100% of state transitions and node executions persisted. 4) Critic precision/recall ≥ 0.9 on benchmark verification suite. 5) Time-to-onboard new node type by 3rd-party developer ≤ 1 day. 6) p95 end-to-end latency for ≤10-node DAG ≤ 30s with gpt-4.1-mini. 7) Zero data loss on graceful pause/stop (all checkpoints flushed).
AI Engineer / Agent Operator
US-1

As an AI Engineer, I want to launch a deterministic AI-agent run from an objective and interactively control it (pause / resume / stop / revise), so that I can ship reliable agents without losing work on interruptions and without re-running expensive LLM calls.

1) Engineer writes neuronium.toml and exports NEURONIUM_OPENAI_API_KEY. 2) Runs `neuronium-agent run -o "<objective>" --interactive`. 3) CLI prints a human-readable timeline (plan → nodes → critics). 4) Engineer presses keys to send pause/stop or types a revise payload. 5) On pause, a checkpoint is written; on resume, run continues from latest phase boundary. 6) Engineer inspects rendered HTML artifact and the run summary.
A valid neuronium.toml exists, NEURONIUM_OPENAI_API_KEY is set, blob backend = fs_cas and index backend = sqlite are reachable, no run with the same execution_id is active.
The user invokes `neuronium-agent run -o "<objective>"` (or calls AgentRunner.start(RunRequest(objective=...))) and the orchestrator transitions PENDING → RUNNING.

A RunHandle (trace_id, execution_id, created_at) is returned, the HTN planner produces a DAG, nodes execute in topological order, every transition is appended to the trace, artifacts are written into FS CAS by canonical artifact_id, and the run terminates in COMPLETED with progress=1.0; if any node fails non-retryably the run terminates in FAILED with FailureClass recorded.
RunRequest{ objective: str, constraints: list[str]=[], mode: "batch"|"supervised"|"interactive"|None, metadata: dict={} }; AppConfig loaded from neuronium.toml; environment variables.
RunHandle, RunStatus(state=COMPLETED|FAILED, progress, current_node_ref, message), trace events in index, rendered HTML artifact path, content-addressed artifacts in CAS.
Run state machine: PENDING → RUNNING → (PAUSED ⇄ RUNNING)* → COMPLETED|FAILED|CANCELLED. Each node: PENDING → READY → RUNNING → COMPLETED|FAILED|TIMEOUT|RETRYING|CANCELLED.
Requirement
The system SHALL accept a RunRequest and return a RunHandle synchronously containing trace_id and execution_id generated as canonical UUIDv7-style identifiers.

The HTN planner SHALL convert the objective + constraints into an Action Graph (DAG) of typed nodes (ModelNode, CodeNode, McpToolNode) with declared inputs/outputs and no cycles, validated before execution.

The deterministic executor SHALL execute nodes in valid topological order, respect llm_temperature=0.0 and default_random_seed from determinism config, and persist each transition (NODE_STARTED, NODE_COMPLETED, NODE_FAILED) into the trace store.
Requirement
Determinism: identical (config, seed, inputs) MUST yield identical canonical artifact_id values across runs (byte-equality), targeting ≥ 99% on the test corpus.
Performance: orchestrator overhead per node transition SHALL be ≤ 50 ms p95 on local SQLite/FS CAS (excluding LLM and tool latency).
Reliability: trace and CAS writes SHALL be crash-safe (fsync on commit), so a SIGKILL never produces a partially indexed artifact or torn trace event.
Fill In
A run is in state RUNNING with trace_id T and at least one node has reached a phase boundary checkpoint.

The operator sends ControlCommand(type ∈ {pause, stop, revise, replan, continue, escalate}, payload) via AgentRunner.control(handle, cmd) or interactive CLI keystroke; in the pause/stop case an InterruptRequest(mode="graceful"|"immediate") may be supplied.

The orchestrator performs declarative state-transition → checkpoint → trace decision WITHOUT executing further DAG nodes; on pause, latest resume_context is persisted and exposed via get_latest_pause_context; on continue/resume_run, execution restarts from the latest phase-boundary checkpoint with no duplicate side-effects; on revise/replan, the planner regenerates the affected sub-DAG and appends a CONTROL_APPLIED event.
ControlCommand{ type, payload: dict }, optional InterruptRequest{ command, mode, export_path }; existing RunHandle.
Updated RunStatus reflecting new state (PAUSED/RUNNING/CANCELLED), CONTROL_APPLIED trace event, optionally exported partial trace at export_path, resume_context dict on pause.

Allowed transitions: RUNNING → PAUSED (pause), PAUSED → RUNNING (continue/resume_run), RUNNING|PAUSED → CANCELLED (stop), RUNNING → RUNNING with new sub-DAG (revise/replan), any → ESCALATED on escalate. Invalid transitions are rejected with a typed error.
Requirement
The Orchestrator.apply_control method SHALL be idempotent: replaying the same ControlCommand on the same checkpoint MUST produce the same state and trace decision.
resume_run(trace_id) SHALL restart execution from the last successfully written phase-boundary checkpoint and SHALL NOT re-execute already-COMPLETED nodes.
Requirement
Control latency: time from control() call to RunStatus reflecting new state SHALL be ≤ 500 ms p95 on local backends.
Safety: no control command SHALL ever cause data loss; on graceful pause/stop all in-flight checkpoints MUST be flushed before state transition.
Auditor / Compliance Officer / QA Engineer
US-2

As an Auditor, I want to export and deterministically replay any past agent run from its trace, so that I can verify the agent's decisions, reproduce incidents, and produce evidence for compliance reviews.

1) Auditor receives a trace_id from production. 2) Runs `neuronium-agent` Python API: AgentRunner.export_trace(handle, "jsonl", "trace.jsonl") to obtain a portable trace bundle. 3) Imports the trace into an analysis env (Postgres/SQLite). 4) Calls AgentRunner.replay(trace_id) in strict offline mode → re-executes nodes against recorded artifacts only (no live LLM/tool calls). 5) Compares replay artifacts to original by artifact_id; mismatches are flagged. 6) Reads rendered HTML and critic evidence to assemble an audit report.
A completed (or failed) run with trace_id T exists in the index store, all referenced artifacts are present in CAS, the auditor has read access to both stores.
The auditor calls AgentRunner.export_trace(handle, format ∈ {"jsonl", "json", "zip"}, path) or `neuronium-agent run --trace-id T --export-trace path`.

An ordered, append-only trace file is produced at `path` containing every state transition, node start/finish, control event, critic verdict and artifact_id reference; for "zip" the bundle additionally embeds the referenced artifact bytes; checksum/manifest is included so any third party can re-import the bundle without access to the source store.
RunHandle or trace_id, TraceExportFormat, target file path, optional include-artifacts flag (implicit for zip).
A trace file (jsonl/json/zip) on disk; success boolean; manifest with event count, artifact count, sha256 digests.
Source store remains read-only; no run state changes; export operation appends an EXPORT_PERFORMED audit event referencing the export digest.
Requirement
export_trace SHALL emit events in deterministic chronological order keyed by (trace_id, sequence_no) with no gaps and no reordering.
The exported bundle SHALL be self-describing: include a manifest with neuronium-v1 canonical-json schema id, event counts, and per-artifact sha256 digests so the bundle is verifiable offline.
Re-importing an exported bundle into an empty store SHALL recreate a queryable run that passes the same get_trace_events / read_artifact_json calls byte-for-byte.
Requirement
Throughput: export of a 10k-event trace SHALL complete in ≤ 10s p95 on local SSD.
Integrity: every exported artifact reference SHALL match its sha256-based artifact_id; any mismatch MUST abort the export with a typed error.
Fill In
A trace_id T is available in the local store (or imported from a bundle) and all referenced artifacts are reachable via the configured BlobStore.
The auditor calls AgentRunner.replay(trace_id=T) which runs the executor in strict offline mode.

The DAG is re-executed using recorded inputs only; every ModelNode/McpToolNode SHALL be served from the recorded artifact instead of a live call; each newly produced artifact_id is compared against the recorded one; the run finishes in COMPLETED with a replay_report listing matches, mismatches and any missing artifacts; on any mismatch the run is marked REPLAY_DIVERGED for investigation.
trace_id, optional replay options (e.g., compare_mode = strict|loose).
RunHandle for the replay run, replay_report{ matched: int, diverged: list[node_ref], missing: list[artifact_id] }, new trace events tagged origin="replay".
A new replay run is created with state RUNNING → COMPLETED|FAILED; original run is untouched; replay events are appended with parent_trace_id pointing to T.
Requirement
Strict replay mode SHALL block any outbound network call (LLM, MCP, web) and SHALL raise NetworkAttemptInReplayError if a node attempts one without a recorded artifact.
The replay engine SHALL surface a structured replay_report and SHALL classify divergences as TRANSIENT (timestamp-only), PERSISTENT (output bytes differ), or CRITICAL (DAG topology differs).
Requirement
Reproducibility: ≥ 99% of recorded runs in the regression suite SHALL replay byte-identical given identical config and CAS contents.
Isolation: replay SHALL run in a sandboxed process with no write access to the production index store; only a dedicated replay_index is mutated.
US-3

As a Platform / Backend Developer, I want to deploy Neuronium in production with Postgres + Redis + RQ workers and pluggable critics/MCP tools, so that I can run many agent jobs concurrently with horizontal scalability, persistent storage, and policy-grade verification.

1) DevOps provisions Postgres (with pgvector) and Redis. 2) Updates neuronium.toml: storage.index_backend="postgres", queue=redis, llm.provider, optional embeddings. 3) Runs `pip install -e ".[all]"` on worker images. 4) Starts API/control plane using AgentRunner; starts N workers with `neuronium-agent worker`. 5) Submits jobs through Python API or CLI; jobs are enqueued to RQ; workers pick them, execute DAG, write to shared CAS+Postgres. 6) Developer plugs in custom MCP tools and custom critics, configures recovery policies, and monitors progress via trace_event_listener / dashboards.

Postgres ≥14 with pgvector, Redis ≥6, shared blob storage (FS or object) are reachable; neuronium.toml points to them; Docker is available for CodeNode sandbox; required MCP tool servers are running and authenticated.

An operator deploys ≥1 control-plane process and ≥N worker processes (`neuronium-agent worker`), then submits RunRequests at rate R via the public API; workers concurrently consume RQ jobs and execute DAG nodes; custom critics validate outputs; recovery policies handle TRANSIENT failures.

The system SHALL distribute work without duplicate execution (RQ job dedupe by execution_id), persist all artifacts/trace into the shared backends, isolate CodeNode execution in a Docker sandbox, surface health and progress via trace_event_listener, and degrade gracefully (DLQ + recovery policy) on PERSISTENT failures while keeping CRITICAL failures from cascading across runs.
neuronium.toml with storage=postgres + queue=redis, env vars (DB URL, REDIS_URL, OPENAI_API_KEY), optional MCP tool registry, optional custom critic and recovery-policy modules.
Per-job RunHandle, persisted runs/artifacts/trace_events in Postgres, indexed embeddings (if pgvector enabled), worker logs/metrics, rendered HTML artifacts, RQ job statuses.

Cluster-wide: each run owned by exactly one worker at a time (lease-based); Postgres tables (runs, artifacts, trace_events, artifact_index, embeddings) updated atomically per phase boundary; Redis holds queues + leases.
Requirement

The system SHALL support pluggable backends behind two interfaces (BlobStore, IndexStore) with at minimum: blob = fs_cas; index = sqlite, postgres; selection driven by neuronium.toml without code changes.

The system SHALL provide a worker entry point (`neuronium-agent worker`) that consumes RQ jobs, leases runs by execution_id, executes the DAG, and releases the lease on terminal state; orphaned leases MUST expire and be re-claimable.
Requirement
Scalability: linear throughput up to 32 concurrent workers on the standard benchmark DAG (≥ 0.85 scaling efficiency); no global locks on the hot path.

Security: CodeNode SHALL execute in a Docker sandbox with no host network and read-only mounts of approved inputs; secrets (API keys) are read from env/secret store and never persisted into trace or artifacts.
Fill In

(a) CLI `neuronium-agent` (Click-based) for human operators; (b) Python API `neuronium_agent.api.create_runner()` / `AgentRunner` for programmatic integration; (c) optional rendered HTML artifact viewer for read-only inspection. No first-party Web/Telegram/Slack UI in scope; integrations are left to adopters via the public API and trace_event_listener callback.

CLI commands: `neuronium-agent run`, `neuronium-agent run --interactive`, `neuronium-agent run --trace-id <id>` (resume), `neuronium-agent run --replay <id>`, `neuronium-agent worker`, `neuronium-agent run -v` / `--summary`. Python API entry: `from neuronium_agent.api import create_runner`. Config entry: `neuronium.toml` + env var `NEURONIUM_OPENAI_API_KEY`.

Timeline view (default): plan → node-by-node progress → critic verdicts → final summary. Verbose view (-v): stdout/stderr per node + critic evidence. Summary view (--summary): execution_id, trace_id, totals, durations, terminal state. Interactive view: keystroke control (p=pause, s=stop, r=revise). Worker view: job-pull log, lease info.

Input: RunRequest JSON-compatible dict { objective: str, constraints: list[str], mode: "batch"|"supervised"|"interactive", metadata: dict }; ControlCommand { type, payload }; CLI flags. Output: RunHandle + RunStatus DTOs; trace files in jsonl|json|zip; artifacts addressed by canonical artifact_id (sha256-based); rendered HTML; structured logs.
Fill In

(1) Orchestrator/Control Plane (in-process AgentRunner facade); (2) Planner Service (HTN → DAG); (3) Execution Engine (deterministic DAG executor); (4) Node Runtime (ModelNode, CodeNode in Docker sandbox, McpToolNode); (5) Trace Service (recording + export + replay); (6) Verification Service (critics: demo, generic, business); (7) Memory Service (GraphRAG-lite + agentic retrieval); (8) Storage Service (BlobStore + IndexStore); (9) Queue Service (Redis + RQ workers); (10) Recovery Service (policies for TRANSIENT/PERSISTENT/SYSTEMIC/CRITICAL).

Orchestrator: state machine, control protocol, checkpoints. Planner: produce typed DAG from objective+constraints. Executor: topological execution, retries, timeouts, determinism. Node Runtime: actual side-effect (LLM, code, MCP). Trace: append-only event log + replay. Verification: pre-commit critic gates with evidence. Memory: store/retrieve graph + vector context. Storage: persist blobs (CAS) and structured rows (runs/artifacts/events). Queue: distribute jobs across workers. Recovery: classify failures and decide retry/escalate.

Plan→Execute→Verify→Adapt loop: 1) AgentRunner.start writes a RUN row, calls Planner, persists DAG. 2) Executor walks the DAG, for each node it gathers inputs from CAS, calls Node Runtime, validates output via Critic, computes canonical artifact_id, writes blob + index rows, appends trace events. 3) Control commands hit the Orchestrator state machine; pause/stop write a checkpoint and gate further execution. 4) On failure, Recovery classifies and either retries or escalates; on critic FAIL, the node is rejected and revise/replan is suggested. 5) replay_run feeds recorded artifacts to nodes instead of live calls.

Public Python facade `AgentRunner` (Pydantic v2 DTOs): start(RunRequest, *, on_handle_ready=None) → RunHandle; get_status(handle) → RunStatus; control(handle, ControlCommand) → RunStatus; resume_run(trace_id) → RunHandle; export_trace(handle, format, path); replay(trace_id) → RunHandle; get_latest_pause_context(trace_id); read_artifact_json(artifact_id); get_trace_events(trace_id); get_latest_rendered_artifact_path(trace_id). Factory: create_runner(config=None, *, trace_event_listener=None). Internal contracts: BlobStore (put/get/exists by artifact_id), IndexStore (insert/query runs/artifacts/trace_events), NodeProtocol (run(inputs)->outputs), CriticProtocol (verify(outputs)->Verdict).

RunRequest{ objective: str (required), constraints: list[str]=[], mode: Literal["batch","supervised","interactive"]|None, metadata: dict[str,Any]={} }. ControlCommand{ type: Literal["continue","pause","revise","replan","stop","escalate"], payload: dict[str,Any]={} }. InterruptRequest{ command: Literal["pause","stop"], mode: Literal["graceful","immediate"]="graceful", export_path: str|None }.

RunHandle{ trace_id: str, execution_id: str, created_at: datetime }. RunStatus{ state: Literal["PENDING","RUNNING","PAUSED","COMPLETED","FAILED","CANCELLED"], progress: float|None, current_node_ref: str|None, message: str|None }. NodeStatus{ status: Literal["PENDING","READY","RUNNING","COMPLETED","FAILED","TIMEOUT","RETRYING","CANCELLED"] }. FailureClass{ kind: Literal["TRANSIENT","PERSISTENT","SYSTEMIC","CRITICAL"], message: str, retryable: bool }. TraceExportFormat ∈ {"jsonl","json","zip"}.

Typed hierarchy in `neuronium_agent.errors`: ConfigError (bad/missing config), PlanningError (invalid HTN/DAG), ExecutionError (node-level, wraps FailureClass), ControlError (illegal state transition), TraceError (corrupt/missing event), StorageError (CAS/index IO), VerificationError (critic FAIL), ReplayError (incl. NetworkAttemptInReplayError, ArtifactMissingError). Strategy: fail fast at boundaries, classify via FailureClass, persist failure into trace, apply recovery policy (retry with backoff for TRANSIENT, escalate/halt for CRITICAL); never lose a partial checkpoint.
Fill In

Run(execution_id PK, trace_id, state, created_at, updated_at, mode, objective, metadata_json), Plan(plan_id PK, run_id FK, root_dag_id), Node(node_ref PK, run_id FK, type ∈ {model,code,mcp}, status, inputs_artifact_ids[], output_artifact_id), Artifact(artifact_id PK = canonical sha256, mime, size, blob_ref, created_at), TraceEvent(seq PK, run_id FK, ts, kind, payload_json, parent_trace_id NULL), Checkpoint(checkpoint_id PK, run_id FK, phase_boundary, resume_context_json), CriticVerdict(verdict_id PK, node_ref FK, critic_name, result ∈ {PASS,FAIL,WARN}, evidence_artifact_id), MemoryNode(id PK, kind, content_artifact_id, embedding vector(pgvector)), MemoryEdge(src FK, dst FK, relation), McpToolRegistry(tool_id PK, endpoint, schema_artifact_id), JobLease(execution_id PK, worker_id, expires_at).

Run 1—1 Plan; Plan 1—N Node (DAG topology in Plan/Node tables, edges in NodeEdge(src_node_ref, dst_node_ref, run_id)); Run 1—N TraceEvent; Run 1—N Checkpoint; Node 1—N CriticVerdict; Node N—1..M Artifact (inputs and one output); Artifact 1—N MemoryNode (optional); MemoryNode N—N MemoryNode via MemoryEdge; Run 0..1—1 JobLease (only one worker leases at a time).

Level 0: User → CLI/API → AgentRunner → {Planner → Executor → Nodes → Critics} → Storage{BlobStore, IndexStore} → Trace; trace_event_listener fan-out to subscribers. Level 1 (per node): Executor pulls input artifact_ids from IndexStore, fetches blobs from CAS, invokes Node (LLM/Code/MCP), receives output bytes, computes canonical artifact_id (sha256 over canonical JSON), writes blob to CAS, inserts Artifact row + Node update + TraceEvent rows transactionally per phase boundary, runs Critic, on PASS commits, on FAIL applies recovery. Replay: same flow but Node Runtime reads recorded output blob from CAS instead of calling external service.

1) User objective + constraints (CLI/API). 2) neuronium.toml config. 3) Environment variables / .env (NEURONIUM_OPENAI_API_KEY, DB/REDIS URLs). 4) MCP tool servers (external). 5) Web fetch tool (HTTP). 6) Recorded trace bundles (for replay). 7) Memory store (prior runs' artifacts + embeddings). 8) Custom user-supplied node, critic, recovery-policy plugins.
Local/dev: 1 dev machine, Python 3.11+, 2 vCPU / 4 GB RAM / 10 GB disk; SQLite + FS CAS in `.neuronium/`; outbound HTTPS to OpenAI; optional Docker Desktop for CodeNode sandbox.

Production (small cluster): control-plane node 2 vCPU / 4 GB; 2–8 worker nodes 4 vCPU / 8 GB each (Docker engine on each for CodeNode); Postgres ≥14 with pgvector extension (4 vCPU / 16 GB / 200 GB SSD); Redis ≥6 (2 vCPU / 4 GB); shared object/blob storage or NFS for FS CAS (500 GB+); egress to OpenAI / MCP tool endpoints.

Software/runtime: Python ≥3.11; pydantic 2.x; click 8.x; openai 1.x–3.x; python-dotenv; beautifulsoup4; optional extras: psycopg (postgres), redis + rq (queue), pgvector, sentence-transformers + numpy (embeddings), docker SDK (sandbox).
Storage: BlobStore=fs_cas (content-addressable on-disk) and pluggable; IndexStore ∈ {sqlite, postgres}; migrations under `neuronium_agent/storage/migrations/`.
Networking: HTTPS egress to LLM provider; intra-cluster TCP to Postgres/Redis; Docker network isolation for CodeNode (no host network by default); MCP tool endpoints reachable from workers.
Observability: structured logs from CLI/API; trace events as the source of truth; pluggable trace_event_listener for Prometheus/Loki/OpenTelemetry adapters built by adopters.

Security: API keys via env / secret manager (never persisted to trace/artifacts); Docker sandbox for CodeNode (no host net, read-only mounts); Postgres role separation (RW for workers, RO for auditors); replay runs write only to a dedicated replay_index.

CI/CD: pytest + pytest-asyncio + coverage suite covering canonical JSON, config loading, storage, deterministic reproducibility; Hatchling build backend; pip-installable via extras `[docker], [postgres], [redis], [pgvector], [embeddings], [dev], [all]`.
Backups & DR: nightly pg_dump of IndexStore; CAS snapshotting (rsync/object-store versioning); trace bundles (zip) act as portable, self-verifying backups per run.

column2: Fill In
UC-1.1 (Start a deterministic agent run)

Build the foundational Plan→Execute pipeline end-to-end so a user can call `neuronium-agent run -o "<objective>"` (or `AgentRunner.start`) and obtain a COMPLETED run with persisted artifacts, trace and HTML render. Includes: HTN planner → DAG, deterministic executor with topological order and seed/temperature controls, ModelNode (OpenAI provider) + minimal CodeNode + McpToolNode stub, BlobStore=fs_cas with sha256-based canonical artifact_id, IndexStore=sqlite with migrations for runs/artifacts/trace_events/nodes/edges, base trace events (RUN_STARTED, NODE_STARTED, NODE_COMPLETED, RUN_COMPLETED), public Pydantic DTOs (RunRequest, RunHandle, RunStatus), AgentRunner.start / get_status, AgentRunner factory create_runner(), neuronium.toml loading + env override, CLI command `neuronium-agent run` with timeline / -v / --summary modes.
None (this is the foundation; assumes OpenAI API key access for live LLM tests).

(a) `neuronium-agent run -o "demo objective"` ends in COMPLETED on a clean install with default config; (b) two consecutive runs with identical config and seed produce byte-identical artifact_id values for all nodes ≥ 99% of the time on the regression suite; (c) trace contains an event for every state transition and every node start/finish; (d) HTML artifact path is returned by get_latest_rendered_artifact_path; (e) unit + integration tests green; coverage ≥ 80% on touched modules; (f) docs CONFIG_SPEC.md + PUBLIC_API_SPEC.md updated.
Description
column4: Acceptance Criteria

column2: Implement HTN planner that converts (objective, constraints) into a typed DAG of ModelNode/CodeNode/McpToolNode with declared input/output contracts; validate acyclicity and input-completeness before returning the plan.
column4: Given a fixture objective, the planner returns a DAG whose `topological_sort` succeeds, all node inputs are produced by some upstream node or are run-level inputs, no cycles, and the produced plan is identical across runs with the same seed.

column2: Implement deterministic executor: topological execution, llm_temperature=0.0, default_random_seed propagation, FS CAS write of every output, canonical artifact_id (sha256 over canonical JSON), SQLite migrations & inserts for runs/artifacts/trace_events/nodes/edges, structured logging.
column4: On the regression suite, two runs with identical inputs/seed produce byte-identical artifact_ids in ≥ 99% of nodes; SQLite schema migrates from empty DB to current version idempotently; crash test (SIGKILL during write) leaves no torn artifact (verified by sha256 check on next start).

column2: Implement public surface: AgentRunner.start / get_status / get_latest_rendered_artifact_path / get_trace_events / read_artifact_json, factory create_runner(), CLI `neuronium-agent run` with default + -v + --summary outputs and graceful Ctrl+C handling.
column4: `pytest tests/api` covers happy path + error path; `neuronium-agent run -o "..."` prints the timeline and a final summary line containing trace_id, execution_id, state=COMPLETED; -v prints stdout/stderr per node; --summary prints aggregate durations/counts.

column2: Fill In
UC-1.2 (Pause / Stop / Resume / Revise running agent)

Add the declarative Control Protocol on top of T-1: explicit run state machine (PENDING/RUNNING/PAUSED/COMPLETED/FAILED/CANCELLED), checkpoints at phase boundaries, ControlCommand handling (continue/pause/revise/replan/stop/escalate), InterruptRequest support, AgentRunner.control / resume_run / get_latest_pause_context, idempotent Orchestrator.apply_control, interactive CLI mode (keystroke control), CONTROL_APPLIED trace events, typed ControlError on illegal transitions.
T-1 (planner + executor + state persistence + trace must exist).

(a) Pause→Resume round-trip on a long-running test node restarts from latest phase-boundary checkpoint with no duplicate side-effects; (b) p95 latency from control() call to RunStatus reflecting new state ≤ 500 ms on local backends; (c) Replaying the same ControlCommand on the same checkpoint is a no-op (idempotency test); (d) Illegal transitions raise typed ControlError without mutating state; (e) Interactive CLI keystroke handlers ship with help text and integration test; (f) Graceful pause/stop flushes all in-flight checkpoints before transitioning.
